texto: An objection was made that judging what had already been done to me, if we proceeded some of us would most certainly be killed. And several others, each in their own language, countered that the way we were living here, the way we were treated, we might as well be dead already, that things couldn't get worse. Things can always get worse, someone yelled from the back, when you reach hell there is always another hell underneath.

texto: There is pleasure in reading a version of myself I know in my heart could never exist, since mine is not an iron mind coldly calculating every possible option and outcome. Instead I am a businessman who loves excitement, loves tension, loves risk and the unexpected, and just happens to possess an extraordinary, on occasion even miraculous, degree of good luck.

texto: He tells me he can't believe how bitter he has become, how sour, that all his life he had been one of the most fun, one of the happiest, one of the most joyous people anyone knew, and now he was like a crumpled piece of steel covered in rust. The way he describes himself, a 'crumpled piece of steel covered in rust,' I don't think I'll ever forget those precise words or his voice as he said them.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jacob Wren: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “He tells me he can't believe how bitter he has become, how sour, that all his life he had been on…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, betrayal, curdled-love pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de curdled-love e disillusionment.

texto: I want to give a name to my would-be killer. What should I call him? Something that will ease his presence in my mind, make him look foolish, like he is of no threat and never was, which is in fact the truth. I don't want his real name, which is meaningless to me, but instead something I control, something I own, some way to own that piano-idiot who attacked me.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jacob Wren: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I want to give a name to my would-be killer. texto: If we make a union in these fields, is there anything we can do to ensure it doesn't become corrupt? Or that later it doesn't only look after the people who work here, we just look after our own, and everyone else can fend for themselves? We need to fight for ourselves, here and now, but we also need changes so large and impossible they encompass the entire world.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jacob Wren que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “If we make a union in these fields, is there anything we can do to ensure it doesn't become corru…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre trabalho, abuse-of-power, class-war, com foco em mudanca e termino.

texto: Friendship is a strange idea, difficult to quantify and, at times, even more difficult to maintain. Clearly a friend is someone you enjoy spending time with. However, a friend is also someone you continue to support even during periods when they are considerably less pleasurable to be around. The loyalty of friendship often contains a kind of tautology or feedback loop: the longer you are friends the more loyal you become, and the more loyal you become the longer you remain friends.
explicacao: Citação de Jacob Wren sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Friendship is a strange idea, difficult to quantify and, at times, even more difficult to maintai…” articula amizade, transactional-relationship, friendship em registro de amizade, com relevância para contextos como termino e transactional-relationship.
